1.title: No single leader
1.text: Judges opens after Joshua's death: with no leader over all Israel, each tribe had to take possession of its own inheritance.

1.question: Which tribe did the Lord say should go up first against the Canaanites?
What did Adoni-bezek acknowledge after his thumbs were cut off?
options: That God repaid him as he had done to other kings

1.question: Who took Kirjath-sepher and received Achsah as wife?
Why did Judah fail to drive out the inhabitants of the valley?
What did Israel do with the Canaanites when it grew strong, instead of driving them out?
Which tribe did the Amorites force into the mountains, not letting them come to the valley?

2.title: What a 'judge' is
2.text: In this book a 'judge' does not preside over courts: he is a deliverer raised up by God to save Israel from oppressors and to lead it.

2.question: What did the angel of the Lord rebuke at Bochim?
options: Israel's leagues and not throwing down the altars

2.question: How old was Joshua, the servant of the Lord, when he died?

2.template: they forsook the Lord, and served Baal and {0}

2.question: What happened repeatedly after a judge died?
options: The people corrupted themselves and followed other gods

2.question: Why did the Lord leave nations undriven-out?
options: To prove whether Israel would keep his way

3.title: 'The land had rest'
3.text: Each cycle ends with a refrain: after the judge delivers Israel, 'the land had rest' for 40 or 80 years — until the next relapse.

3.question: Which peoples did the Lord leave to prove Israel and teach it war?
Who was the first judge, who defeated Chushan-rishathaim?
How many years did Israel serve Eglon king of Moab?
How did Ehud kill Eglon king of Moab?
options: With a two-edged dagger hidden on his right thigh

3.question: Why did Eglon's servants delay before entering the parlour?
options: They thought he was relieving himself

3.question: Who killed six hundred Philistines with an ox goad?

4.title: The only female judge
4.text: Deborah is the only woman among Israel's judges — a prophetess who judged the people under a palm tree and led the nation to victory.

4.template: {0}, a prophetess, the wife of Lapidoth, she judged Israel

4.question: What did Deborah tell Barak, who would only go to war if she went too?
options: The honor of the victory would go to a woman
The Lord would not go with him

4.question: Whom did the Lord deliver into Barak's hand at mount Tabor?
Who hid Sisera in her tent and gave him milk?
How did Jael kill Sisera while he slept?
options: She drove a tent stake through his temple

5.title: 'A mother in Israel'
5.text: In the song Deborah calls herself 'a mother in Israel.' Chapter 5 is poetry — one of the Bible's oldest passages — retelling the victory of chapter 4.

5.question: What is Judges chapter 5?
options: A song of Deborah and Barak celebrating the victory

5.question: According to the song, what brought war to Israel's gates?
Whom does the song call to awake and utter a song?

5.template: the stars in their courses fought against {0}

5.question: Whom did the angel of the Lord command to be cursed for not coming to help?
Who, at the song's end, looked out the window waiting for the chariot that never returned?

6.title: Hiding at the winepress
6.text: Gideon was threshing wheat hidden in a winepress, fearing Midian, when the angel greeted him as a 'mighty man of valour.'

6.question: Who oppressed Israel, coming like locusts to destroy the harvests?
What did God send to the people before raising up Gideon?
How did the angel of the Lord greet Gideon?
options: 'The Lord is with thee, mighty man of valour'

6.question: What confirmed to Gideon that he spoke with the angel of the Lord?
options: Fire rose from the rock and consumed the offering

6.question: What did Gideon do by night at the Lord's command?
options: He tore down his father's altar of Baal

6.question: What did the men of the city demand on learning Gideon had torn down the altar?
options: That he be put to death
That he rebuild the altar in gold

6.question: What came upon Gideon so that he gathered the army?
What sign did Gideon ask with the fleece of wool?
options: The fleece wet and the ground dry, then the reverse
That the sun would stand still
That water would turn to blood

7.title: 300 against a multitude
7.text: From 32,000 men God reduced Gideon's army to just 300 — so the victory would clearly be his, not Israel's strength.

7.question: Why did the Lord have Gideon reduce the army?
options: So Israel could not vaunt that its own hand had saved it

7.question: How were the 300 men chosen?
options: They were those who lapped the water with the hand

7.question: What dream of a Midianite encouraged Gideon?
options: A cake of barley bread that overturned the tent

7.question: With what did the 300 attack the camp of Midian?
options: Trumpets and pitchers with torches inside

7.question: Which princes of Midian were slain in the pursuit?

8.title: Gideon refuses the throne
8.text: Gideon refused to be king, saying only the Lord should rule over Israel — yet the golden ephod he made ended up leading the people astray.

8.question: Which tribe complained at not being called to the battle against Midian?
What did the men of Succoth deny Gideon's weary soldiers?
Which two kings of Midian did Gideon capture?
What did Gideon do to Succoth and Penuel?
options: He punished them for refusing help
He made them cities of refuge

8.question: What did Gideon answer when they wanted to make him king?
options: 'I will not rule, nor my son; the Lord shall rule over you'

8.question: What did Gideon make from the spoil's gold, which became a snare to Israel?
How long did the land have rest in Gideon's days?

9.title: Killed by a millstone
9.text: Abimelech died when a woman cast a piece of a millstone on his head; wounded, he asked his armourbearer to kill him, so none could say a woman slew him.

9.question: What did Abimelech do to his seventy brothers to become king?
options: He killed them on one stone

9.question: In Jotham's parable, what were the trees seeking?
options: A king to reign over them

9.question: In Jotham's parable, which plant agreed to reign over the trees?
What did Jotham wish, in his curse, between Abimelech and the men of Shechem?
options: That fire would come out and consume them mutually
That they would have long peace
That they would grow rich together

9.question: How long did Abimelech rule over Israel?
Who rose up in Shechem and defied Abimelech?
Who warned Abimelech of Gaal's revolt in Shechem?
What did Zebul tell Gaal about what he saw coming down the mountains?
options: That they were men coming down to battle
That they were shadows of the mountains

9.question: What happened to Gaal after the battle?
options: He was driven out of Shechem

9.question: How did those who took refuge in the tower of Shechem die?
options: Abimelech burned the tower over them

9.question: To which tower did Abimelech go to attack next, where he would meet his death?
How does the book sum up Abimelech's death?
options: God requited the wickedness he did to his brothers

10.title: The minor judges
10.text: Tola and Jair are 'minor judges,' told in only a few verses. Jair had thirty sons and thirty cities, called Havoth-jair.

10.question: Which judges are named at the start of chapter 10?
Israel's idolatry led to oppression by which peoples in this chapter?
What did the Lord answer, with irony, when Israel cried out again?
options: 'Go and cry unto the gods which ye have chosen'

10.question: What did Israel do before the Lord's soul was grieved for their misery?
options: It put away the strange gods and served the Lord

11.title: A rash vow
11.text: Jephthah promised to offer whatever first came out of his house when he returned in victory — and it was his only daughter who came out to meet him with timbrels.

11.question: Why did Jephthah's brothers drive him from home?
options: Because he was the son of another woman

11.question: What did the elders of Gilead ask of Jephthah?
options: To be their head in war against the Ammonites

11.question: What did Jephthah do before warring against the Ammonites?
options: He sent messengers trying to settle it in peace

11.question: According to Jephthah, whose land had Israel taken when it left Egypt?
What argument of time did Jephthah use against the Ammonites?
options: That Israel had dwelt in the land three hundred years
That the war would last seven years
That he would make peace forty years
That he would wait ten years

11.question: What vow did Jephthah make to the Lord before the battle?
options: To offer whatever first came out of his house to meet him

11.question: Who came out first to meet Jephthah, tragically fulfilling the vow?

12.title: The password 'Shibboleth'
12.text: The Ephraimites could not make the 'sh' sound and said 'Sibboleth' instead of 'Shibboleth' — which exposed them at the fords of the Jordan.

12.question: What word did the Gileadites use to identify the fleeing Ephraimites?
Elon and Abdon, named at the chapter's end, were what?

13.title: The Nazarite vow
13.text: Samson was to be a Nazarite from the womb: no wine, no touching anything unclean, and no cutting his hair — an outward sign of his consecration to God.

13.question: Who oppressed Israel for forty years when the angel announced Samson?
What rule would govern the boy's life, according to the angel?
options: He would be a Nazarite: no wine and no razor on his hair
He would be a priest in the temple
He would be king of Israel
He would live in the desert

13.question: What did Manoah ask of the Lord after the announcement?
options: That the angel return to teach them to raise the boy
That the son be born a king

13.question: What did the angel answer when Manoah asked his name?
options: That he was the Lord himself

13.question: What name was given to the boy who would begin to deliver Israel?

14.title: The lion and the honey
14.text: Samson killed a lion with his bare hands; later he found a swarm of bees and honey in its carcass — which inspired his wedding riddle.

14.question: What did Samson ask of his parents when he went down to Timnath?
What did Samson find in the carcass of the lion he had killed?
options: A swarm of bees and honey

14.question: What riddle did Samson pose at the feast?
options: 'Out of the eater came forth meat, and out of the strong came sweetness'

14.question: How did the Philistines learn the answer to the riddle?
options: They pressed Samson's wife to draw it out of him
They read it in a book

15.title: Thirst after victory
15.text: After killing a thousand Philistines, Samson nearly died of thirst; God opened a spring of water, and the place was called En-hakkore, 'the spring of him that cried.'

15.question: How did Samson destroy the Philistines' crops?
options: He loosed foxes with torches tied to their tails

15.question: What did the Philistines do to Samson's wife and her father in revenge?
options: They drove them from the city

15.question: What did the men of Judah do to Samson to hand him over to the Philistines?
options: They bound him with new cords
They hid him in a cave

15.question: With what did Samson kill a thousand Philistines?

16.title: The hair that grew back
16.text: In prison, blind, Samson's hair began to grow again — and God gave him strength one last time for his avenging death.

16.question: What did Samson do with the gates of the city of Gaza?
options: He tore them off and carried them to the top of a hill

16.question: What did the Philistine lords ask of Delilah?
options: To find out the secret of Samson's strength
To kill him in his sleep

16.question: How many times did Samson deceive Delilah before revealing the secret?
What was the secret of Samson's strength?
options: His never-cut hair, the sign of the Nazarite vow

16.question: Where did the Philistines gather to celebrate Samson's capture?
How did Samson kill more Philistines in his death than in his life?
options: By pulling down the temple's pillars upon them all
By setting the temple on fire

17.title: 'Every man did what he wanted'
17.text: The refrain 'there was no king in Israel; every man did that which was right in his own eyes' sums up the whole appendix of the book (chapters 17–21).

17.question: What did Micah do with the silver he returned to his mother?
options: He had a graven image made
He gave it to the temple
He gave it to the poor

17.question: What phrase sums up this disordered period of Israel?
options: 'There was no king in Israel; every man did what was right in his own eyes'

17.question: Whom did Micah hire to be the priest of his private shrine?

18.title: The landless tribe
18.text: Dan had not yet taken possession of its inheritance; so it sent spies looking for a land to conquer.

18.question: What was the tribe of Dan seeking in this chapter?
What did Dan's spies find at Micah's house?
options: A Levite as priest and the idols

18.question: How did the spies describe the city of Laish?
What did Dan's 600 men carry off from Micah's house?
options: The image, the ephod and the Levite

18.question: What did Micah do when he pursued the Danites who robbed him?
options: He went home, for they were too strong

18.question: What did the Danites do with Laish?
options: They took it, burned it and called it Dan
They made an alliance with it

19.title: No king, no law
19.text: This dark account opens again with 'in those days there was no king in Israel,' showing the moral collapse when every man did as he pleased.

19.question: Where did the Levite go to bring back his concubine?
What did the father-in-law do to detain the Levite in Beth-lehem?
options: He urged them to stay another day

19.question: Why did the Levite refuse to lodge in Jebus (Jerusalem)?
options: Because it was a city of strangers, not of Israelites

19.question: Who took the Levite in at the street of Gibeah when no one received him?
What happened that night in Gibeah?
options: Wicked men surrounded the house and committed a great evil
There was a feast of peace

19.question: What did the Levite do to call all Israel to justice?
options: He divided the body and sent the parts to the tribes
He wrote a letter to the king

20.title: Inquiring of the Lord at Beth-el
20.text: After two defeats, Israel fasted and inquired of the Lord before the ark at Beth-el, where Phinehas served — only then came the victory.

20.question: Where did all Israel gather over the crime of Gibeah?
What did Israel demand of the tribe of Benjamin?
options: To hand over the wicked men of Gibeah

20.question: What did Benjamin do instead of handing over the guilty?
options: It gathered for war against Israel

20.question: What happened on the first day of battle?
And on the second day of battle against Benjamin?
What did Israel do after two defeats in a row?
options: It fasted and inquired of the Lord at Beth-el

20.question: What strategy did Israel use on the third day?
options: It set an ambush around Gibeah
It besieged the city by famine

20.question: What was the agreed signal of the ambush against Gibeah?
options: A pillar of smoke rising from the city

20.question: What happened to the army of Benjamin?
How many Benjamites escaped to the rock of Rimmon?

21.title: Saving a tribe
21.text: Israel had sworn not to give its daughters to Benjamin; so the tribe wouldn't vanish, it had to find a way to provide wives for the 600 survivors.

21.question: What problem arose after the war against Benjamin?
options: The tribe nearly vanished and Israel had sworn not to give them their daughters

21.question: What did Israel do with Jabesh-gilead, which had not joined the army?
options: It took young women from it as wives for Benjamin

21.question: What did the surviving Benjamites still lack?
What solution did they find at the yearly feast of Shiloh?
options: The Benjamites would seize girls who danced at the feast
They would buy wives in Egypt

21.question: How does the book of Judges end?
options: 'There was no king in Israel; every man did what was right in his own eyes'
